MerlinGame.tscn.notes: Root is plain Node, relies on MerlinGameUI sub-scene for all rendering

MerlinCabinHub.tscn.children: none visible in .tscn - all created by script
MerlinCabinHub.tscn.notes: Minimal scene file. Script creates all UI. Known CRITICAL parse error in merlin_cabin_hub.gd blocks loading.

scene_grep_results.notes: No background_color or modulate properties set directly in .tscn files

root_cause: MerlinGame.tscn and MerlinCabinHub.tscn use plain Node as root (not Node2D/Control). Rendering depends entirely on scripts and sub-scenes creating visual nodes at runtime. MerlinCabinHub has a known parse error in its script, preventing the scene from loading at all. If the sub-scene MerlinGameUI.tscn fails to load or its script errors, the screen would be blank. The lack of any background ColorRect in the .tscn files means there is no fallback color — the window clear color (project settings) would show through.
next_steps: 2. Add a CanvasLayer + ColorRect(black/dark) as first child in MerlinGame.tscn and MerlinCabinHub.tscn via editor
3. Set project-level window/background_color in project.godot to a dark color as fallback
4. Verify MerlinGameUI.tscn has proper background coverage
5. Test scene transitions Menu3DPC -> MerlinCabinHub -> MerlinGame in editor
verdict: DIAGNOSED - root cause identified, fix requires Godot editor for scene hierarchy changes
